title: West Gopura, Angkor
highlights:
This causeway crosses the huge moat that encircles the Angkor Wat complex. On reaching the West Gopura, the  ...    The West Gopura is most people's first sight of Angkor Wat. It comprises a main prasat, or lotus tower, flanked on either sides by smaller prasats. All three of these prasats are in various stages of decay, but have since been restored, not not rebuilt to their original forms.## Angkor Wat ison theMap of Angkor ## West Gopura

title: The fortification of Angkor Wat | Antiquity | Cambridge Core
highlights: Abstract Famous for its role as a Vishnuite temple during the twelfth and thirteenth centuries, Angkor Wat's subsequent fate has attracted less interest. Traces of modifications to the outer walls of the complex may, however, hold the key to understanding its role during its later phases. Here, holes in the masonry and structural changes to the substantial walls are investigated to demonstrate how wooden structures with a defensive role were built to protect the site  ...
